text.#hero-title: Make confident decisions about solar, battery storage and energy performance in Morocco
text..hero-copy > p:not(.hero-badge): Atlas Energie Conseil helps businesses, property owners, farms, hotels, schools, developers and project owners assess profitability, compare technical options and move forward with an independent view.
text.#situations .section-heading p:not(.eyeb: We step in when an energy decision needs to be clarified before you commit budgets, partners or a deployment timeline.
text..situation-card:nth-of-type(1) p: Identify cost-reduction levers and rank the actions that are genuinely worth studying.
text..situation-card:nth-of-type(2) p: Review photovoltaic potential, economic assumptions and the criteria for a sound decision.
text..situation-card:nth-of-type(3) p: Clarify the value of storage based on your usage profile, consumption profile and resilience objectives.
text..situation-card:nth-of-type(4) p: Strengthen operational continuity for sensitive, industrial, agricultural or logistics sites.
text..situation-card:nth-of-type(5) p: Structure the studies, scenarios and trade-offs before making a financial commitment.
text..situation-card:nth-of-type(6) p: Get an external, rigorous and neutral view before validating a solution or partner.
text.#situations .section-cta p: Do you have an energy decision to clarify?
text.#systems-title: Which system is right for your site?
text.#systemes .section-heading p:not(.eyebro: Every site has a different consumption profile, risk level and investment logic. Atlas Energie Conseil helps you compare scenarios before you commit to a technical or financial solution.
text..system-card:nth-of-type(1) .system-desc: A photovoltaic option focused on reducing electricity costs and improving self-consumption.
text..system-card:nth-of-type(1) .system-deta: Businesses or sites with regular daytime consumption that want to reduce costs without adding unnecessary system complexity.
Consumption profile, solar potential, self-consumption rate, profitability, site constraints and economic consistency.
text..system-card:nth-of-type(1) .system-note: Usually worth studying when consumption naturally matches solar production hours.
text..system-card:nth-of-type(2) .system-desc: A scenario combining solar generation and storage to improve self-consumption, flexibility and peak management.
text..system-card:nth-of-type(2) .system-deta: Sites with demand peaks, flexibility needs, partial autonomy objectives or a clear interest in storage.
Battery sizing, usage cycles, economic trade-offs, peak reduction, expected resilience and operating limits.
text..system-card:nth-of-type(2) .system-note: Relevant only when the battery serves a measurable use, not as a default technical add-on.
text..system-card:nth-of-type(3) .system-desc: A hybrid architecture for sites where power continuity, backup or energy resilience is a critical issue.
text..system-card:nth-of-type(3) .system-deta: Industrial, logistics, agricultural or isolated sites exposed to outages, grid constraints or continuity requirements.
Critical loads, backup strategy, solar-storage-generator coordination, security levels, operating costs and fallback scenarios.
text..system-card:nth-of-type(3) .system-note: Worth analyzing when the main issue goes beyond energy savings and affects business continuity.
text..system-card:nth-of-type(4) .system-desc: An independent mission to clarify options before committing to a solar, storage or hybrid project.
text..system-card:nth-of-type(4) .system-deta: Decision-makers, investors and project owners who need to test assumptions before approving a budget, selecting a partner or planning deployment.
Consumption, objectives, profitability, technical risks, compared scenarios, investment priorities and next steps.
text..system-card:nth-of-type(4) .system-note: Recommended when the decision is not mature yet or several options are competing.
text.#systemes .section-cta .button: Compare options with an independent view
text.#pre-diagnostic-title: Clarify your need in a few questions
text.#pre-diagnostic .diagnostic-copy > p:not: Answer a few simple questions to identify the scenario worth exploring. This pre-diagnostic does not replace a technical study; it prepares a structured first discussion.
text..diagnostic-assurance p: No personal data is requested, no responses are stored, and the result is not a technical or financial guarantee.
text..diagnostic-result-note: This guidance is indicative. It is not a complete technical or financial study. A discussion helps verify assumptions and frame the next steps.
text.#services-title: Strategic advisory for companies and project owners
text.#services .section-heading p:not(.eyebro: We work across the energy decision chain to turn technical, financial and operational questions into clearer choices.
text..services-grid .info-card:nth-of-type(1): Diagnosis, usage optimization, action ranking and a performance roadmap adapted to your site and assets.
text..services-grid .info-card:nth-of-type(2): Opportunity studies, technical framing, sizing review and preparation for robust photovoltaic deployment.
text..services-grid .info-card:nth-of-type(3): Structuring battery options around flexibility, resilience and the optimization of energy assets.
text..services-grid .info-card:nth-of-type(4): Project coordination, maturity review, investment preparation and stakeholder alignment up to key decisions.
text.#apropos-title: An independent approach to structure the right energy decisions.
text.#about-title: A practical, independent approach for energy decisions that need to be clear, rigorous and directly usable.
text..about-copy > p:nth-of-type(2): Atlas Energie Conseil supports Moroccan businesses and project owners who want to secure their choices, improve energy competitiveness and develop solid solar or storage projects.
text..about-copy > p:nth-of-type(3): Our approach connects technical issues, execution constraints and investment logic so projects can move forward with method, discipline and clarity.
text..about-pillar:nth-of-type(1) span: A structured view of energy, operational and investment priorities.
text..about-pillar:nth-of-type(2) span: Concrete, robust recommendations adapted to the local Moroccan context.
text.#secteurs-title: Environments where energy becomes a competitiveness lever.
text.#sectors-title: Advisory calibrated for demanding sites and investment decisions
text.#secteurs .section-heading p:not(.eyebro: Our missions are designed for energy-intensive companies, service-sector sites, critical infrastructure and project developers.
text..sector-card:nth-of-type(1) p: Energy performance, cost control and integration of renewable solutions at site scale.
text..sector-card:nth-of-type(2) p: Asset value, energy modernization and a clearer view of realistic improvement potential.
text..sector-card:nth-of-type(3) p: Operational continuity, energy resilience and the structuring of complex technical projects.
text..sector-card:nth-of-type(4) p: Support for feasibility, project preparation and the maturation of investment decisions.
text.#avantages-title: A clear method to turn energy questions into well-framed decisions.
text.#advantages-title: A demanding method designed to clarify decisions and support execution.
text..reason-card:nth-of-type(1) p: We connect technical, economic and operational issues to provide a complete, usable assessment of the project.
text..reason-card:nth-of-type(2) p: Each mission is framed around your site objectives, internal constraints and business objectives.
text..reason-card:nth-of-type(3) p: Concrete, prioritized recommendations that your teams and partners can use in practice.
text.#faq .section-heading p:not(.eyebrow): Before committing to a solar, storage or hybrid project, some questions need to be clarified. These answers help you understand the stakes before a first discussion.
text.#checklist .checklist-copy > p:not(.eyeb: A concise checklist to identify what to clarify before comparing solar, storage or hybrid offers in Morocco. It does not replace a technical study, but it helps prepare a more structured decision.
text.#checklist .checklist-note: Available by WhatsApp or email after a first message. No complex form required.
text.#checklist .checklist-preview h3: What the checklist helps you clarify
text..checklist-preview li:nth-child(2): Consumption profile: daytime, night, continuous or variable
text..checklist-preview li:nth-child(5): Need for power continuity or backup
text..checklist-preview li:nth-child(6): Oversizing risks or premature technical choices
text..checklist-preview li:nth-child(7): Information to prepare for a structured first discussion
text.#contact-title: Clarify your energy decision in a confidential first exchange.
text.#contact .contact-copy > p:not(.eyebrow): We review your need, clarify priorities and identify concrete next steps for your solar, storage or energy performance project.
text..contact-note p: A direct, confidential and decision-oriented framework to understand your context, constraints and the trade-offs to secure.
text..contact-card a[data-analytics-event='co: Share your context and prepare a structured first exchange.
Start quickly with a prepared message.
text..footer-brand p: Independent advisory in solar energy, storage and energy performance in Morocco.
text..footer-meta > p: (c) 2026 Atlas Energie Conseil. All rights reserved.
text.#mentions-legales .legal-modal-panel > p: Atlas Energie Conseil is an informational and professional contact website for companies and project owners in Morocco.
text.#politique-confidentialite .legal-modal-: Information sent by email, WhatsApp or LinkedIn is used only to handle your request and remains strictly within our professional exchanges.

bill.label: How would you describe your electricity bill?

consumption.label: When does the site mainly consume energy?

battery.label: Is battery storage already being considered?

stage.label: What stage is the project at?

analysis.title: Independent energy analysis recommended
analysis.text: Your project would benefit from independent framing before comparing technical solutions or supplier offers.

pv.title: PV without battery to study
pv.text: A photovoltaic scenario without battery could be a good first option to review, especially if consumption is naturally aligned with solar production.

battery.title: PV + battery scenario to compare
battery.text: A PV + battery scenario is worth comparing if the real usage profile, the sizing and the economic logic are verified.

backup.title: Resilience / backup to assess
backup.text: Your priority appears linked to continuity of activity. A resilience and backup analysis is recommended before making a decision.

exchange.title: First exchange recommended
exchange.text: A first exchange will help clarify assumptions, compare scenarios and prepare a more secure decision.

items.question: When does a solar project become relevant for a business or site in Morocco?
items.answer: A solar project becomes relevant when the consumption profile, electricity costs, available roof or land area and site constraints make coherent self-consumption possible. The analysis should review profitability as well as technical and operational limits.

items.question: Is a battery always necessary with a photovoltaic installation?
items.answer: No. A battery is relevant only if it serves a clear usage profile: shifting energy, reducing certain peaks, improving flexibility or strengthening resilience. It should be sized around the site's real needs, not added automatically.

items.question: What is the difference between solar self-consumption and battery storage?
items.answer: Self-consumption means directly using the solar electricity produced on site. Storage keeps part of that energy for another time or a specific usage profile. The two approaches can complement each other, but they do not answer the same need.

items.question: Why carry out an energy analysis before requesting quotes?
items.answer: An independent analysis helps clarify objectives, compare scenarios and avoid basing the decision only on a commercial offer. It frames the project before approving a budget, selecting a partner or planning deployment.

items.question: How can the power supply of a sensitive or industrial site be secured?
items.answer: Power continuity depends on critical loads, sensitivity to outages, grid conditions, and the possible role of a battery or generator. A structured approach compares backup and resilience scenarios before a decision.

items.question: Does Atlas Energie Conseil install solar panels or batteries?
items.answer: No. Atlas Energie Conseil acts as an independent advisor. The aim is to analyze, compare, structure and secure energy decisions. The support is not based on selling or supplying equipment.

items.question: Who does Atlas Energie Conseil support?
items.answer: The support is designed for businesses, homeowners with structured projects, farms, hotels, schools, service-sector sites, industrial companies, developers, investors and project owners who want to clarify a solar, storage or energy performance decision in Morocco.

items.question: What happens during the first exchange?
items.answer: The first exchange is used to understand the site context, objectives, constraints, project maturity and decisions to secure. It identifies useful next steps without immediately committing to a full study.

items.question: What information is useful to analyze an energy site?
items.answer: Useful information may include electricity bills, consumption profiles, site constraints, available roof or land area, continuity objectives, offers already received and investment priorities. The information available helps define the right level of analysis.

items.question: Does the pre-diagnostic replace a technical study?
items.answer: No. The pre-diagnostic provides indicative first guidance. It does not replace a complete technical, economic or financial study. It prepares a more structured exchange and identifies the scenario to explore.

schema.professionalDescription: Independent advisory in solar energy, battery storage, energy performance and project structuring in Morocco.
